title: glaucocarpum suffrutescens
intro: Glaucocarpum suffrutescens, also known as wood's rose or western rosy sage, is a perennial plant native to the western United States and Canada, characterized by its fragrant, pink or white flowers and edible berries. Traditionally, Glaucocarpum suffrutescens has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Glaucocarpum suffrutescens include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and support immune function. Glaucocarpum suffrutescens can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve, making it a versatile herb for various herbal preparations.
